sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Sharon, presents with right shoulder pain that began approximately three weeks ago after a fall from a ladder while painting.
She reports that she was able to get up and continue working but experienced some pain, which she managed with ice and ibuprofen.
The pain has been intermittent and is currently rated as a 6 out of 10 without medication, which decreases to a 4 with ibuprofen.
The patient reports no numbness or tingling in the arm or hand.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: The patient is currently taking ibuprofen for pain management.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has limited active and passive range of motion of the right shoulder, with tenderness to the greater tuberosity of the humerus.
There is no tenderness at the sternoclavicular joint.
The patient has good strong bilateral hand grips, brisk capillary refill of less than three seconds, and equal and bilateral response to sensation and touch.
Test Results: The right shoulder x-ray reveals no fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are most likely due to rotator cuff tendinopathy, with injury to the tendons of the muscles that make up the shoulder.
Problem list: Rotator cuff tendinopathy; active.
Plan:
I ordered an MRI of the right shoulder to rule out any other underlying conditions.
I referred the patient to physical therapy for six to eight weeks.
I recommend the patient continue to take an NSAID if tolerated and take Tylenol for pain management.
The patient will follow up in two weeks to evaluate the effectiveness of the treatment plan.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up in two weeks to evaluate the effectiveness of the treatment plan and make any necessary adjustments.
